key_points: Garrigus dorm furniture replaced with tiny, barely-functional desks
The Standard off-campus housing has persistent, unfixed wifi issues
Housing waitlist forces seniors into expensive off-campus leases with no backup
key_quotes: 'I lived in Garrigus last year and found out they replaced the furniture with this shitty furniture... I was pissed.'
'Our wifi is still bunz even though they said they fixed it... 1350 a month'
'Rescinding guaranteed housing screwed me over... I got a lease at Knollwood because I might not have been taken off the waitlist until August'

key_points: Commuters and transfers report severe isolation; friend groups form in dorms
Campus vibe described as 'apathetic' and 'empty' by multiple students
Basketball culture is dominant; everything else feels secondary
key_quotes: 'I can't help but feel like this place is so apathetic if that makes sense. Like, there's not much life here.'
'Transferring as a junior kinda cooked me in making close relationships, it seems like everyone already has their groups'
'I'm a freshman here and have no one to talk to... I'm ready to drop out already because of how lonely I am here'

key_points: Dining hall hygiene incidents reported, including unsanitary food handling
Food variety complaints surface regularly; students post about missing specific items
Campus has beloved off-campus spots that students mourn when they close
key_quotes: 'I saw a supervisor scratching his balls for like 10 seconds and immediately start to handle silverware'
'There's no broccoli at south, I keep needing to eat cauliflower'
'that place and their garlic parm tenders carried me through my degree' (on Wings Over closing)

key_points: Isolation is a recurring theme, especially for commuters and transfers
'Windy town in the middle of nowhere' contributes to depression for some students
University offers therapy dogs during finals, but structural loneliness persists
key_quotes: 'Being in this windy ass town in the middle of nowhere is really weighing on me'
'College has made me kinda depressed... something just feels so off about being here'
'Ngl I'm lonely at UConn... I'm ready to drop out already because of how lonely I am'

key_points: UConn does not meet 100% of demonstrated financial need for most students
Out-of-state costs hit $37-40k/year; OOS students question the value
Merit scholarships exist but are primarily reserved for Connecticut residents
key_quotes: 'My FAFSA is -1500 and I was expecting 45k in aid but I have to pay 39,000 dollars, that's 7k more than my guardian makes in a year'
'UConn be like welcome to the Husky nation! Now you have the opportunity to go into debt for 40 years and take out 150k in student loans'
'Why would the guy from MA pay 37K to go to UConn. Just go to UMass as a state resident and pay a fraction of that.'

key_points: Engineering and civil engineering programs have strong job placement reputations
Classes are genuinely rigorous; workload surprises students expecting an easy ride
Grade data is now publicly available to check professor difficulty before enrolling
key_quotes: 'No one told me this but if you're a civil engineer major you will very likely easily find a job after graduating'
'The classes are not easy. I made a schedule of readings, discussions, and papers.'
'Worst part was all the papers, 8 page, 16 page and 32 page papers and for some reason 1 1/2 space instead of double space'

key_points: Administration removed trash cans from offices campus-wide to cut costs
UConn deleted alumni email accounts without warning, breaking professional networks
Title IX handling drew public callout from a student, with community support
key_quotes: 'UConn is cutting costs by removing trashcans from all offices in buildings. That's how our budgeting is going.'
'They deleted my entire account including OneDrive... all my professional communications were done with my uconn.edu email'
'UConn doesn't give a fuck about you... they deleted even that. Without telling us.'

key_points: Storrs is genuinely isolated; students need a car to access anything off-campus
Weather is brutal: cold, windy, and punishing for a large walking campus
Campus is architecturally growing but the surrounding town remains underdeveloped
key_quotes: 'There is actually nothing to do except workout... one of the guys had to bring the car from home just so we can get out of this town'
'The wind and the cold are terrible. And it's a big campus, so it can be a long cold walk to class.'
'I came here and it was kind of a culture shock, there is actually nothing to do'

key_points: Civil engineering grads report strong, near-guaranteed job placement
Business majors struggle more; internship timelines are competitive and stressful
One junior business student documented a successful internship search starting mid-June
key_quotes: 'All my civil friends got jobs right out of undergrad... meanwhile I'm a business major and can't get a job since graduating'
'I'm a Junior business student and started applying in mid-June, just got my offer today'
UConn graduates reportedly earn about $15k less starting salary than comparable private school peers

key_points: In-state with merit aid is genuinely good value; without aid it's questionable
Out-of-state cost rivals private schools without the corresponding prestige bump
Tuition has risen sharply; one post mentions a proposed 30% in-state hike
key_quotes: 'I go to UConn and they are going to be raising tuition for in-state residents by 30%, I'm really not sure if it's worth it'
'I can officially afford to go to one of the best public universities in the country' (after financial aid appeal succeeded)
'UConn will cost me 26k/year as I got no scholarship money... UVM will cost me 43k'

key_points: Basketball dominates campus identity; non-sports students may feel left out
Strong regional loyalty from Connecticut students; others feel it's a fallback
Quirky, self-aware community with real school spirit beneath the complaints
key_quotes: 'uconn is NOT a real place and I will not be gaslit into believing it is... you drive there and it's just trees, more trees, a cow maybe, then suddenly a NATIONAL CHAMPIONSHIP BANNER materializes out of the fog'
'Storrs UConn campus is like its own world... enjoy the process, enjoy the struggle'
'I really want to go (like really really bad) but it's so much, how are they doing this'

key_points: Sexual assault: path between campus and Celeron apartments nicknamed 'The Rape Trail' for decades
Administration misconduct: UConn wrongly arrested a professor, then settled after cover-up exposed
Financial aid bait: net price calculator predictions routinely mismatch actual aid offers
key_quotes: 'Celeron Path... known as the Rape Trail for decades' — confirmed in Daily Campus reporting
'UConn got professor Sherry Zane arrested on criminal larceny... the UConn investigator's report was a cover-up of UConn's illegal compensation practices'
'I was expecting UCONN to reward me at least 45,000 in financial aid according to the calculator but alas, I have to pay 39,000 dollars'

key_points: Underground steam tunnel system keeps power on during major storms when CT loses electricity
UConn Botanical Conservatory has the largest witch's broom dwarf conifer collection in North America
Honors program is selective and genuinely opens doors; worth pursuing if eligible
key_quotes: 'This steam system is why I never lost power in my dorm from 2011-14 despite major weather events causing weeklong outages across the state'
'The UConn Botanical Conservatory's Corpse Flower is opening this evening... the first at UConn in over ten years'
'I got my BA and MA at UConn in 2022 and 2023 and I just got into my first PhD program at Kent State'
